name: GO Feature Flag
pricing_notes: Fully open source (MIT); self-hosted; no cloud service fees

auth.notes: Optional API key for relay proxy; config-file based for embedded mode

performance.notes: In-process evaluation is sub-millisecond; relay proxy adds ~1ms; no external service call

agent_readiness.notes: Lightweight OpenFeature-compatible feature flag system. File-based flag config (YAML/JSON/TOML). Relay proxy for polyglot apps. Supports boolean, string, number, JSON flags. Percentage rollouts, user targeting, A/B testing. No database required — flag config in file/S3/GCS/GitHub. OpenFeature SDK compatible. Multi-language SDKs via relay proxy.

gotchas: File-based config means no real-time UI for flag management (use relay proxy + exporter)
Less feature-rich than commercial platforms (no built-in analytics)
Flag changes require config file update or reload — no instant toggle UI
best_when: Teams wanting OpenFeature-compatible self-hosted feature flags with zero vendor lock-in
Simple feature flagging without complex analytics or experimentation needs
GitOps teams storing feature flag configs in git (YAML in repo)
Cost-sensitive teams avoiding commercial feature flag SaaS
avoid_when: Teams needing built-in experimentation analytics (use GrowthBook or Statsig)
Non-technical stakeholders needing self-service flag management UI
Complex targeting rules requiring rule builder UI
summary: GO Feature Flag is a lightweight OpenFeature-compatible feature flag system. File-based flag configuration (YAML/JSON stored in file/S3/GitHub). Relay proxy for multi-language support. Percentage rollouts, user targeting, A/B testing. Sub-millisecond evaluation. No database required. Best for teams wanting GitOps-friendly self-hosted feature flags compatible with the OpenFeature standard.
